items.prompt: interprets sequences, series, partial sums, and local polynomial approximations in bounded BC-calculus contexts
items.expected_evidence: interpret sequences, series, and partial sums consistently across tables, formulas, graphs, and verbal descriptions
items.scoring_guidance: Meets when the learner treats BC-level series and approximation evidence as coherent limiting behavior rather than as disconnected procedures.

items.prompt: explains how convergence, divergence, and Taylor-style approximation arise from the structure of terms, partial sums, and local behavior
items.expected_evidence: explain why convergence or divergence claims follow from the structure of terms and accumulated partial behavior
items.scoring_guidance: Meets when the learner explains BC-level convergence and approximation as coherent structure-driven reasoning.

items.prompt: Diagnose a peer error aligned to taylor_local_model_breakdown.
items.expected_evidence: connect Taylor polynomial approximations to local function behavior and error-sensitive reasoning
items.scoring_guidance: Meets when the learner identifies where a polynomial approximation stopped matching the intended local behavior or error expectation.

items.prompt: interprets a less-familiar BC-calculus context and identifies which convergence, approximation, or advanced-change evidence governs the claim
items.expected_evidence: justify BC-level calculus claims in bounded modeling or analysis tasks using the correct convergence, approximation, or advanced-change evidence
items.scoring_guidance: Meets when the learner preserves coherent BC-calculus reasoning in a varied context and names the evidence that actually controls the conclusion.
